name: Doyle Canning
subject: My Endorsements & Oregon Voter's Guide
body:
If you supported me, trust me on this: Biden simply must crush Trump by a landslide in Oregon 04. Not just to make a statement about how we feel about Trump, but to lay the groundwork for the future - for the generational change that everyone knows is com | |
---
|
---
Dear friends and supporters, Many of you have been reaching out to ask, "Will you be making endorsements for the November election?" And today the answer is, Oh Yes! This year, I urge you to...
1. Vote early - Don't wait till Election Day!
2. Use a ballot drop box ([click here to find yours](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTJup836pU-BL_IxqhEV6ocfI517icdI4b7doAHhWQMh2eLAc76EgsP_R6k1yBoWFeuHUWNizBUGt_k3LS10iF5bcLuwBwdDWLndaAiXRD8oh-mTlXoVRMCE2c8jfGMrzV-2ccahQhyRbtG4UUowwoKquv6_gpUL_f4nc_mCdanTqwzmDGfWtS42xfVjEFhULHRPKUDIJZArGmj8TMiBM9L9jbYU_zwwSq11BMnbk-YGoO7tZ4fQcDrXxKL5-VeH61WxGf0ZqfpDjFJWMZuH1s8xRBoFoMhzbhJpp3J1zKc7-wb0xWmVzbkjsUiKELd25QA/364/WOLsERO0Q4qJuE7pLSRfpg/h0/BEmzI4Pc5S4RFpe9ZghfxPMFPkkigSkGP-ZyGzKXwXo))
3. Definitely Vote Democrat up and down the ticket!

But before you mark your ballot, check out my voters guide below, some endorsements I've made, and join me in supporting these candidates today. 2020 has been difficult for so many reasons - the pandemic, politics, job losses, and social isolation - but I haven't lost sight of the urgency of progressive change and the absolute necessity of removing the most corrupt, lawless, racist, and inept administration we've ever known. I supported Senator Bernie Sanders to lead our party to the presidency, and I ran for Congress on a platform that was more aligned with Elizabeth Warren's agenda than that of Joe Biden. But ballots are in the mail, and it's time to vote for who is on the ballot, and start to build for the future. Progressives can and must show up in record numbers, and we can make the difference in ways that will keep candidates accountable and engaged with our priorities...but only if we vote and are vocal about our support! Here's how I urge you to vote:
---
| [ ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/BqA3LhzOFl92KwaVoz69N8TuG-kFRkOoDRIm3AUAUi7xj-pSoJs6sHqlrWpHuNdVUGHwf-e4FR5PDLWVpiz6HPeYSCkrPTnE5u3GfTiXHyS4QSkXg-jaoIr6m3gMAwxHLHRIDmkD5bEJpCqBOfiX0LQ17wgEpvli24Sv_iczSgTj4NlI6nHfS41MRRthtilP0FNGxO6t2BQFkqV5wZVyjc5VppwEv-MmnKxzbx1Vi3TvqUZSlu76J7prLppwZNBkgtmJbAqqjxjCAWtwFd8O9ckIBi8HxnpLmdgkTFvq_07l5VNQqVtfEBqUS3G8dbFWE4WluepKNx_jNGrUMP0eWLbYeunf8Fa42lr6wvzCDDbasiSAE-8KAft50hTtmNIrHuyvU_WiWrs4-31fmMBQ2A/364/WOLsERO0Q4qJuE7pLSRfpg/h1/paRwqcFiEdzEaKo2uTD1SCZO1KbzqWAEM-fEom0QFtM)
---
I joined Springfield's own Spent the Rent Podcast to discuss my slate... It's a fun conversation about what's at stake in this election: [Watch on Facebook ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDPAZiEt8wwS6hp5wOIb3CiJOLoRNZhJkmRhvVKKULthGBakDgW8Bkt1GT5HPm8YW2EO2L-8SWp-tr4ag8_POUAuJEglvkDQLJAjVv-2gK_v2HJq34VfRGSnE3_H6TobS_J3k7YemuLvHqzwMclrPr01W7prgdxxnywFaZUxCVlCDXwXqzfmmeZREpI9NSwTa5Z5hk2O8sAInMXMqbx-1fEyKLBzUc5TKGn4QyU2aA9sNw61JLSmybzNwdeCWa5BUvCQ8lLfHM2Tpl0UkcBxaunmtTuXkFVpuiJuBgIMw5lYLD_4T8xO_tuRYHO8WfFt7ng/364/WOLsERO0Q4qJuE7pLSRfpg/h2/G52T77PQOZNbFjPLKvNbE-8mgrq5N7FUfqjD0ZWn2pU) [Listen on Apple Podcasts](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/1XQbiUZqIgkFRQxQopJGKeqdhWuIqpW0eQhmA8EUWy9uxahRNFrW2bPOxw_ps1rj7Dv-fvlcqK5yDxN9PSCLxTftw2XsaggVso62nKiZvSj3tqpvUOmhmBepWn2eBIQ0GXB70jLybVGVP4MJEGgUUDbnqMKHVS4IxjGeJmwxQMkgK1kllbOGvt_33j8c5GyhOVKa6hurcbcCVIIwkCIqH-ipk9likz-bcNBx2J8iQ0kiMinCKJ8khhRSSnqITAmoSxapQOTzvqTG3qG3q7c5nxIpVTUvqp0yhb0m3babM6kRLsnrpRoCDeNcf1-vMJWX2Bih24OIOfeK7UppykOPKlXvFGdaLAM-8eemz6tfWmZUnNesAkQuA99YVcjKHZW5/364/WOLsERO0Q4qJuE7pLSRfpg/h3/98sxROohjZte6D4lTQMwLVgToz3garF4nZNUYarrNNU) [Listen on Spotify ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/BqA3LhzOFl92KwaVoz69N1o820IF1CZXMXClxNRaI5FEtF0wmFY8ouFFsiEXpMbGs-ljZPY4zDegGdJd0j9efJd-R7_fBnHLFNWusDLO2WmIHz7SJfgFB7t4ZCn1A-4LrNz0bB65sDQqRnF-YBlzI4uwKukTEDsBQsFTQehsf1sVabuUHEX4ShSwFbHmUe9YieDvKQBKnhmwTgRk8bKcoEfb2vu1MHrGMINd2nMoju1F-MFMTaBUFtnopyoQxOd9oHfWgD_a03XnOJp23V5RUX6iWjSXDycpRVjLQte4nBRZUdhIRApKa9JflfaMrfpJmkzHpQCOUjfYS1sOZFvYklcPEKIDBlFTdSTANWptVHcphkgx7NnH2-k8cJ8_BoTT/364/WOLsERO0Q4qJuE7pLSRfpg/h4/qHjYRL9VteLRq6ErjuPBT1KbwJhhOz-c6dPA8Fml4Fc)
---
**President: **Vote Joe Biden/Kamala Harris. If you're on this email list, these two probably weren't your top picks for president. I want you to know that I am all in. Joe convened task forces with Bernie that effectively unified our party, making genuine steps to the left on issues like climate, immigration, and policing. I know many of my supporters may not feel like its enough, and you're 100% not wrong. But as Joe likes to say, "Here's the deal": Progressive operatives called our Congressional District "too risky" for a challenger because of the too-close-for-comfort margin for Clinton vs. Trump in 2016. They preferred "deep blue" districts where Clinton dominated by double digits in '16 -- even though many of these were districts where she also prevailed in the primary, indicating a less favorable terrain for progressives than Oregon 04, where Sanders romped. We tried to explain to national politicos that the '16 result was too close because roughly 20,000 of you wrote in Bernie or voted Green, because of a deep anti-establishment sentiment in our community, and a palpable dislike for the Clinton campaign. We showed them the numbers, and the map, arguing that an energized electorate in Eugene and Corvallis can and will carry the district for a true-blue progressive. But the '16 Clinton margin was just too close. This caused prominent allies that wanted to back our campaign to back away, and it really hurt us. If the Biden/Trump margin is close, it will further entrench the notion that we can never have a progressive Democrat in Congress, because it is not a "safe" district for Democrats. That is why I'm telling you to vote for Joe and I'm telling you to be loud about it! We need to put Trump in the trash with double digits. It may seem counter-intuitive, but a vote for Biden today is a vote for a future path to power for progressives in the future. **If you supported me, trust me on this: Biden simply must crush Trump by a landslide in Oregon 04.** Not just to make a statement about how we feel about Trump, but to lay the groundwork for the future - for the generational change that everyone knows is coming. I am urging you, if you want to see investments in young progressive candidates here, a landslide victory for the Biden-Harris ticket in Oregon 04 this November is necessary. Your vote is not a valentine, it is a chess move, and this is a long game we have to win. Checkmate baby, Let's go!
---
**Senate:** Jeff Merkley (I) Ahh, after all that inside baseball about Biden, Merkley for Senate is an endorsement I feel really good about! 100% Yes on Senator Merkley. His leadership on climate, the child separation (aka kidnapping) crisis at the border, and his most recent vocal opposition to normalizing the installation of Amy Coney Barrett on the Supreme Court, show me who he is as a Senator, and that he is leading on the high stakes fights in Washington. He's also a really kind person, and the kind of white guy Senator who thought about running for president and decided not to because somebody has got to play defense in the Senate for us here in Oregon. That is humility, and good governance. Thank you Jeff!
---
**Congress: **Peter DeFazio (I) You may not want to accept our loss in May, but I am asking you, urging you, **vote DeFazio for Congress**. I've seen some supporters on social media saying they will write in my name for Congress, and I am touched, but please - for the reasons stated above re: Joe Biden, a resounding defeat for the Republican challenger is absolutely necessary right now. **The 17,700 votes our campaign claimed in the primary will be a difference maker in the margin of victory, and we want to see a slam dunk for the Democrat, Peter DeFazio.** Even though no one at the Defazio campaign has ever reached out to me, I'm a Democrat, and I am making my endorsement crystal clear to my supporters. Just as Bernie has rallied his millions of supporters to get behind Biden, and am asking my thousands of supporters to get behind Defazio. The more we show up for Defazio right now, the more power we have the push our Congressman on our priorities, in what will hopefully be a trifecta legislating moment for Democrats in 2021. Let's crush this thing!
---
**Secretary of State: **Shemia Fagan I supported rural Oregonian and environmentalist Jamie McCloud-Skinner in the primary for this seat, because of her No Corporate Money campaign and strong stance against Jordan Cove. (Jamie is also a Law Duck like me, so we share a nice personal connection). But Shemia won it, by an inch, and I'm really glad about it. Enthusiastic about what she'll bring to the role and can't wait to see her take the office from Republican timber-industry operative Bev Clarno next year. Go get 'em Shemia!
---
**Attorney General: **Ellen Rosenblum (I) Ellen was the speaker at my law school graduation, and gave a rousing call to action about using the law to defend the rights of marginalized people and the planet. I'd like to see her do that more often, especially in the battles ahead on Jordan Cove. Vote for Ellen, _and_ push her to fight harder.
---
|
---
**LOCAL RACES...**
---
**Eugene/Springfield** **Matt McRae for EWEB Board** 100% yes! Matt is in the fight for our climate and will bring much needed voice for our planet and people on the utility board. Vote [Matt McRae for EWEB Board](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDPAZiEt8wwS6hp5wOIb3CiJ5sThx4ZQi7s1trP-C6tfDAeq6p3W0u_qSAXPvG8zr1W7KqBgjJQUDc3rDnQvbkAqusD4TfOgjAC0UHzcVEFLrijJZfJr_Ycb7_TRZt7gi7MUGA09KUfWyc2p4aKh4LiM9KEEkhEZZng46h8zxRXYNFEvPHEMgkxz5UumYcZKszzRa4sLuGHoVNqzn_U7Bm0NMlbFCb3RfxJtmefBS8bdjW72dvvnrtDGKBtX0Ep6RArsBOWIVU_UZ8prm7P_QLNNJfuuvYmFlX5A0KQJUEULkWt6C5cH_Ofo6Qpdu_zCC9XZKjJrym-KpZs_aOZgEVew/364/WOLsERO0Q4qJuE7pLSRfpg/h5/Nt6dbOLejyKb_vJP0EB43odN9p7awwnaJRNA4j3iSB0). **Joel Iboa for Lane County Commissioner, South Eugene** A lot of my South Eugene supporters are split on this race between what they see as two equally progressive candidates. I've personally spoken with both candidates about their platforms, and the reckoning on racial justice we've been in as a country and community. That's why I'll be supporting Joel in November. In the wake of the murder of George Floyd, Joel makes clear in his voters pamphlet statement that he will "Re-allocate funds away law enforcement and invest in a community-centered health and safety model." Oversight of the Lane County Sheriff's office and jail is a key part of this job, and racial justice has to be a priority. Joel's leadership on racial justice and police accountability has been consistent and unwavering. We have a connection through the Civil Liberties Defense Center, (he is a board member and I am a volunteer) which has sued the Lane County Jail numerous times for civil rights violations. Joel also knows his stuff on Environmental Justice and climate too. I'm excited that Joel made it to the general, and he's my choice for my next county commissioner. [Phone bank for Joel. ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDIxV2GHztlw1MWzKhEIn4mzkYD532kFhZabMfHRiE2fUhFxhm-eT5XsS8tDKI7U-fWfkktMyUQz_AC5WWP7XTJXh4iUyHlR3qfuTJ8FDy-8CbH9AgphX7bF5hRu5Cw1Yc8IzANhu-ErekjggiWsjYeBmAp-rGtaL4VRtyBVQZmnrEPoI0VDIIoQqHt1OHYuN09_7Kpf_gr1z_Ro9n90eS2Bq6Pijqw2EqLQ8G60OGhPQn55oyBa6bkJO18el5GOJvEeyYb6ZLbIpHlQFfu57i1a5ndKlU2d2AqG50_PRi01T20ttIeyHQ6isMFR5WLxfZg/364/WOLsERO0Q4qJuE7pLSRfpg/h6/c707iwnv89MmMcvYGhOALKsC5YTWbn5TfrWIykPXgBg) **Also, in Eugene - Vote YES for Libraries.** **Johanis Tadeo for Springfield City Council, Ward 3** Johanis is facing off for a spot on the Springfield City Council, Ward 3, and after the events of this summer with footage of police brutality against black demonstrators that echoed newsreel footage of Selma, we need some racial justice leadership at city hall. Johanis has sat with my family around our kitchen table, talking for hours about the change we need to see at the local and global level. He is tireless, sometimes over-committed, and hardly a politician or political operative. Johanis is a true activist, and is deeply connected to the community and committed to challenging the power structure as it stands. I have seen him grow as a leader over the course of this campaign, and the COVID crisis. Johanis has been stepping up to feed and house community members where our local government has failed. Now he needs a seat at the table. clean_body: If you supported me, trust me on this: Biden simply must crush Trump by a landslide in Oregon 04. Not just to make a statement about how we feel about Trump, but to lay the groundwork for the future - for the generational change that everyone knows is com | | | Dear friends and supporters, Many of you have been reaching out to ask, "Will you be making endorsements for the November election?" And today the answer is, Oh Yes! This year, I urge you to... 1. Vote early - Don't wait till Election Day! 2. Use a ballot drop box () 3. Definitely Vote Democrat up and down the ticket! But before you mark your ballot, check out my voters guide below, some endorsements I've made, and join me in supporting these candidates today. 2020 has been difficult for so many reasons - the pandemic, politics, job losses, and social isolation - but I haven't lost sight of the urgency of progressive change and the absolute necessity of removing the most corrupt, lawless, racist, and inept administration we've ever known. I supported Senator Bernie Sanders to lead our party to the presidency, and I ran for Congress on a platform that was more aligned with Elizabeth Warren's agenda than that of Joe Biden. But ballots are in the mail, and it's time to vote for who is on the ballot, and start to build for the future. Progressives can and must show up in record numbers, and we can make the difference in ways that will keep candidates accountable and engaged with our priorities...but only if we vote and are vocal about our support! Here's how I urge you to vote: | I joined Springfield's own Spent the Rent Podcast to discuss my slate... It's a fun conversation about what's at stake in this election: President: Vote Joe Biden/Kamala Harris. If you're on this email list, these two probably weren't your top picks for president. I want you to know that I am all in. Joe convened task forces with Bernie that effectively unified our party, making genuine steps to the left on issues like climate, immigration, and policing. I know many of my supporters may not feel like its enough, and you're 100% not wrong. But as Joe likes to say, "Here's the deal": Progressive operatives called our Congressional District "too risky" for a challenger because of the too-close-for-comfort margin for Clinton vs. Trump in 2016. They preferred "deep blue" districts where Clinton dominated by double digits in '16 -- even though many of these were districts where she also prevailed in the primary, indicating a less favorable terrain for progressives than Oregon 04, where Sanders romped. We tried to explain to national politicos that the '16 result was too close because roughly 20,000 of you wrote in Bernie or voted Green, because of a deep anti-establishment sentiment in our community, and a palpable dislike for the Clinton campaign. We showed them the numbers, and the map, arguing that an energized electorate in Eugene and Corvallis can and will carry the district for a true-blue progressive. But the '16 Clinton margin was just too close. This caused prominent allies that wanted to back our campaign to back away, and it really hurt us. If the Biden/Trump margin is close, it will further entrench the notion that we can never have a progressive Democrat in Congress, because it is not a "safe" district for Democrats. That is why I'm telling you to vote for Joe and I'm telling you to be loud about it! We need to put Trump in the trash with double digits. It may seem counter-intuitive, but a vote for Biden today is a vote for a future path to power for progressives in the future. If you supported me, trust me on this: Biden simply must crush Trump by a landslide in Oregon 04. Not just to make a statement about how we feel about Trump, but to lay the groundwork for the future - for the generational change that everyone knows is coming. I am urging you, if you want to see investments in young progressive candidates here, a landslide victory for the Biden-Harris ticket in Oregon 04 this November is necessary. Your vote is not a valentine, it is a chess move, and this is a long game we have to win. Checkmate baby, Let's go! Senate: Jeff Merkley (I) Ahh, after all that inside baseball about Biden, Merkley for Senate is an endorsement I feel really good about! 100% Yes on Senator Merkley. His leadership on climate, the child separation (aka kidnapping) crisis at the border, and his most recent vocal opposition to normalizing the installation of Amy Coney Barrett on the Supreme Court, show me who he is as a Senator, and that he is leading on the high stakes fights in Washington. He's also a really kind person, and the kind of white guy Senator who thought about running for president and decided not to because somebody has got to play defense in the Senate for us here in Oregon. That is humility, and good governance. Thank you Jeff! Congress: Peter DeFazio (I) You may not want to accept our loss in May, but I am asking you, urging you, vote DeFazio for Congress. I've seen some supporters on social media saying they will write in my name for Congress, and I am touched, but please - for the reasons stated above re: Joe Biden, a resounding defeat for the Republican challenger is absolutely necessary right now. The 17,700 votes our campaign claimed in the primary will be a difference maker in the margin of victory, and we want to see a slam dunk for the Democrat, Peter DeFazio. Even though no one at the Defazio campaign has ever reached out to me, I'm a Democrat, and I am making my endorsement crystal clear to my supporters. Just as Bernie has rallied his millions of supporters to get behind Biden, and am asking my thousands of supporters to get behind Defazio. The more we show up for Defazio right now, the more power we have the push our Congressman on our priorities, in what will hopefully be a trifecta legislating moment for Democrats in 2021. Let's crush this thing! Secretary of State: Shemia Fagan I supported rural Oregonian and environmentalist Jamie McCloud-Skinner in the primary for this seat, because of her No Corporate Money campaign and strong stance against Jordan Cove. (Jamie is also a Law Duck like me, so we share a nice personal connection). But Shemia won it, by an inch, and I'm really glad about it. Enthusiastic about what she'll bring to the role and can't wait to see her take the office from Republican timber-industry operative Bev Clarno next year. Go get 'em Shemia! Attorney General: Ellen Rosenblum (I) Ellen was the speaker at my law school graduation, and gave a rousing call to action about using the law to defend the rights of marginalized people and the planet. I'd like to see her do that more often, especially in the battles ahead on Jordan Cove. Vote for Ellen, and push her to fight harder. | LOCAL RACES... Eugene/Springfield Matt McRae for EWEB Board 100% yes! Matt is in the fight for our climate and will bring much needed voice for our planet and people on the utility board. Vote . Joel Iboa for Lane County Commissioner, South Eugene A lot of my South Eugene supporters are split on this race between what they see as two equally progressive candidates. I've personally spoken with both candidates about their platforms, and the reckoning on racial justice we've been in as a country and community. That's why I'll be supporting Joel in November. In the wake of the murder of George Floyd, Joel makes clear in his voters pamphlet statement that he will "Re-allocate funds away law enforcement and invest in a community-centered health and safety model." Oversight of the Lane County Sheriff's office and jail is a key part of this job, and racial justice has to be a priority. Joel's leadership on racial justice and police accountability has been consistent and unwavering. We have a connection through the Civil Liberties Defense Center, (he is a board member and I am a volunteer) which has sued the Lane County Jail numerous times for civil rights violations. Joel also knows his stuff on Environmental Justice and climate too. I'm excited that Joel made it to the general, and he's my choice for my next county commissioner. Also, in Eugene - Vote YES for Libraries. Johanis Tadeo for Springfield City Council, Ward 3 Johanis is facing off for a spot on the Springfield City Council, Ward 3, and after the events of this summer with footage of police brutality against black demonstrators that echoed newsreel footage of Selma, we need some racial justice leadership at city hall. Johanis has sat with my family around our kitchen table, talking for hours about the change we need to see at the local and global level. He is tireless, sometimes over-committed, and hardly a politician or political operative. Johanis is a true activist, and is deeply connected to the community and committed to challenging the power structure as it stands. I have seen him grow as a leader over the course of this campaign, and the COVID crisis. Johanis has been stepping up to feed and house community members where our local government has failed. Now he needs a seat at the table. Also, in Springfield - Vote yes for fire department, their job is obviously essential! | Southwest Oregon Katie Eymann for Coos County Commissioner Katie has been in the fight against the fracked gas pipeline and Jordan Cove LNG for well over a decade. She is an environmental champion, connected to the community, and knows that the south coast holds the keys to energy transition -- and can build a boom in local jobs -- through renewable offshore wind and regenerative forestry. Katy is smart, humble, and someone I was really grateful to get to know in the primary season. I'm really proud to support . Calla Felicity for House District 1, Curry County Calla is running a tough race for the right reasons. She is challenging one of the lead sponsors of a ban on abortion care, sadly without any support from usual reproductive rights orgs. Calla is a leader on climate justice, healthcare for all, and is running a grassroots campaign with no corporate PAC money. She said, "Every day, I will be working for a Just Transition into the Oregon Green New Deal." Proud to stand with . Jerry Samaniego, House District 7, Lane/Douglas County Jerry is trusted friend and a core supporter of my run for Congress, knocking doors in the rain like a champ. Jerry is fighting back and running hard against one of the Republican cowards who walked out on the job last year when climate legislation was up for a vote in Salem. Jerry is a solar energy installer, rural Oregonian and evacuee of the Holiday Farm fire. He's a proud signer of the No Fossil Fuel Money pledge, and I am so excited to back. Kat Stone for Senate District 1, Douglas/Coos/Curry Counties Kat Stone is another Canning supporter challenging a Republican incumbent in a rural district, reaching from Roseburg down to Brookings and the California border including the breathtaking Klamath-Siskiou. Like most folks down there, she's a hard No on Jordan Cove. Kat is a nurse and has been on the frontlines of the virus outbreaks in rural Oregon and her perspective is sorely needed in Salem. Kat and her family were big supporters of my campaign in Douglas County and represent the fiercely independent and progressive Oregon that holds the keys to job creation in sustainable agriculture, cannabis, forestry, and renewable energy - aka, the future we need and deserve. Help Kat put up a fight against another corrupt Republican who fled the state rather then take a vote on clean energy jobs last year. ! Portland Eugenians don't usually weigh in on Portland politics, but Portlanders certainly do try to shape how things happen here...so here's to flipping the script and telling PDX how to vote ;) Khanh Pham for House District 46 Khanh romped with 90% of the vote in May (unheard of!). She is now running unopposed in November. I am so incredibly proud of my friend and her breathtaking landslide in her primary for HD 46. Khanh has an ambitious vision for what she'll do in Salem, and how she'll use her seat to create and inside-outside strategy for the climate justice movement. Donate to Khanh so she can build the team she needs to make it happen and . Sarah Iannarone for Mayor of Portland The race between incumbent Mayor Ted Wheeler and challenger Sarah Iannarone is very tight, and probably the most exciting thing happening in Oregon politics today. Despite unprecedented progressive challenger campaigns across the state, including our own, only Sarah survived into the general and has a real shot at taking out an incumbent and getting the keys to city hall next year. Now is not the time for a long shot write in campaign for a candidate who is not even running. The latest poll from OPB shows a very close margin and its time to close ranks behind Sarah and get Tear Gas Ted Wheeler out! Sarah is a Gen X mom, and a strong voice for the climate. Some of my favorite aspects of her platform: \- Portland Net zero by 2030, \- no new fossil fuel infrastructure including gas hookups \- ban oil trains. - And a strong focus on racial equity and jobs. Her campaign is truly exciting and I am all in: ! Chris Smith for Metro Chris is another climate leader and vocal opponent of freeway expansion. He also has a cool wheelbarrow bike that his kids ride around in, just like yours truly. He's the one we need in office. A win for Chris is a win for climate. Vote Chloe Eudaly (I) Just stop it with the Portland Police Union backed-challenge to progressive champion Chloe Eudaly. She is a strong voice countering gentrifying developers in city hall, and has the backing of Sunrise Movement PDX for her stances against the Zenith oil terminal and I-5 expansion. Please Portland, Elsewhere Carina Miller for Senate District 30 Carina is an incredible community leader and environmental justice champion from Warm Springs, east of the Cascades. She served as the the Wasco tribe representative on the Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs Tribal Council. Carina's areas of focus were cannabis, environment, energy, education, climate change, and economic development. She was on the workgroup for clean energy jobs bill and spent countless hours advocating for rural communities and just transition. Carina is also on the board of the ACLU of Oregon and is challenging a Republican in deep red eastern Oregon. She is building something truly exciting for the future of rural Oregon, grounded in Indigenous sovereignty. Deb Patterson for Senate District 10 Meeting Deb is like a breath of fresh air. She is warm, smart, and a committed champion to making healthcare a human right for every Oregonian. Deb is a bold progressive fighting to flip this senate district in/around Salem, and her victory will help build the super majority and prevent future walkouts on crucial Democratic priorities. This one is winnable, let's make it happen. . | | | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Canning For Congress 2020, please .

name: =?utf-8?Q?Ben_Ray_Luj=C3=A1n?=
subject: We cannot afford to lose this seat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
#
Team,
Senator Rosen said I could reach out today about my Senate race in New Mexico.
I'm running for Senate because working families deserve a leader who actually cares about their futures -- but Trump and the RNC have had a big red target on this open seat as a potential pickup for Republicans in November, and they're campaigning aggressively to make sure I lose.
**Let me be clear: If Republicans win this race, our chances of flipping the Senate could be off the table.** Flipping the Senate and ending Mitch McConnell's iron grip on our democracy is possible, but even if Democrats can pull off this upset, it'll likely be by razor-thin margins.
That's why I'm asking for your help today. Our party cannot afford to let this Senate seat fall prey to Republicans and their massive fundraising machine -- and a strong Democratic Party is one of the only things standing in the way of that devastating outcome. I'm counting on folks like you to chip in what you can and help me keep this seat blue, because without your help, Mitch McConnell will likely serve another term as Majority Leader.
**[So if you can, will you split a $5 donation between my campaign and Jacky Rosen today? It's the best way to ensure that this Senate seat in New Mexico and so many others nationwide remain in play for Democrats.](https://secure.rosenfornevada.%3D/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-201018-ben-ray-lujan?refcode=em201018i_4676&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=89053&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Henderson&state=NV&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
As president, Joe Biden will move our country forward, but he will need a Senate who will work with him -- not stall and attack his agenda at every turn. For the past several years, Republicans have worked to do little more than win majorities, stymie progress, and give handouts to their wealthy and well-connected friends. That's all about to change.
With your help, candidates from the school board to the Senate to the White House will be able to compete with Republicans, despite their fundraising machine. We've worked for years to build a Democratic Party capable of winning at every level, and all that's left to do now is execute on the strategies that will help us win.
**[But we need your help to get there. So I'll ask once more: If you're able, will you chip in $5 to my campaign and Jacky Rosen today? Momentum is on our side, and I'm counting on folks like you to help us capitalize on it.](https://secure.rosenfornevada.%3D/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-201018-ben-ray-lujan?refcode=em201018i_4676&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=89053&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Henderson&state=NV&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
Thanks for your support,
Ben Ray Lujan

****[Contribute](https://secure.rosenfornevada.%3D/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-201018-ben-ray-lujan?refcode=em201018i_4676&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=89053&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Henderson&state=NV&phone=&employer=&occupation=")****

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409d853a/4fbb2af3/20fb78ff/5e81a2f8/1844712069/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

| Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
---
Contributions to Rosen for Nevada are not tax deductible.

Rosen for Nevada
PO BOX 27195
LAS VEGAS, NV 89126

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dani?=
subject: It's not just numbers
body:
The numbers are staggering. But it’s not just numbers. It’s my nephew, who is being raised in a single-parent household and who, just after his first | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/da061c822688/its-not-just-numbers?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
**$12,378** \-- Average out-of-pocket costs for a family of four with employer-sponsored insurance in 2019.

**37,000** \-- Number of people in IL-16 without health insurance (pre-pandemic!)

**17.1%** \-- Healthcare as a percentage of GDP.

The numbers are staggering. _But it’s not just numbers. _

It’s my nephew, who is being raised in a single-parent household and who, just after his first birthday, had to see a pediatric neurologist for an EEG. It came back normal, but if not for Medicaid, my sister wouldn’t have known whether her baby’s headaches were life-threatening or not.

It’s the granddaughter of a voter I spoke to in Princeton, who was born with a condition that means she will eventually lose her sight and her hearing and, if not for the protections for pre-existing conditions put in place by the Affordable Care Act, she’d also lose her life. She is two.

It’s one of our super volunteers, who needs knee surgery and knows how lucky she is to be able to afford it and get the surgery without having to put it off for years.

It is every single person who had to make a choice between a mammogram and groceries or who skipped their blood pressure medication for a month because their car needed new tires.

**The stories hardly even make us wince anymore because they’re so common. But as with any other injustice, we can’t look away. **

When I’m elected to Congress _(which looks increasingly likely!)_ , I’ll be fighting to increase access to healthcare, lower prescription drug costs, and protect people with preexisting conditions.

[Can you chip in today to help me do that?](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=4264983606&e=140a9b81e7)

Dani

| Corky Messner wants you to join him and Sen. Tom Cotton for lunch! Read more >> Derek, I wanted to send you a quick reminder about tomorrow's lunch with Corky Messner and U.S. Senator Tom Cotton. You can RSVP securely here: _[_**https://secure.winred.com/messner-for-senate/cotton-luncheon**_](https://click.mlsend.com/link/c/YT0xNTM0OTc2MzY5MjcwNzI0MTY3JmM9ZjJiNiZlPTE5MjgmYj00MzM2ODkyNjImZD1kOXk1bThs.pDrViGtoQgCh_kXhqCR8hFjZR97rPrszwcc2rbcD3mA)_ Best, Elliot |
---
Derek,
Corky Messner is hosting a luncheon with Senator Tom Cotton on Monday, October 19th and wants you to join!
[ ](https://click.mlsend.com/link/c/YT0xNTM0OTc2MzY5MjcwNzI0MTY3JmM9ZjJiNiZlPTE5MjgmYj00MzM2ODkyNjUmZD1xNWo4YzB3.T7aWiwty-saW8EyfY5SZgV6kxm8-f_oqtXEtY3kLMiM)

**Where** : The Grand At Bedford Village Inn
**When** : Monday, October 19th, 12:00 P.M. - 1:15 P.M.
**RSVP** : [_https://secure.winred.com/messner-for-senate/cotton-luncheon_](https://click.mlsend.com/link/c/YT0xNTM0OTc2MzY5MjcwNzI0MTY3JmM9ZjJiNiZlPTE5MjgmYj00MzM2ODkyNjgmZD1hMXM5aDRs.SBUGuCKWgGXRoTArpS9s-F8AY6b2va2onZGgYDFbewU)
Corky is a West Point graduate, veteran, proven businessman, and principled Conservative running for U.S. Senate against Jeanne Shaheen.
Corky and Sen. Cotton align on many of the most important issues facing our country like economic freedom and personal liberty. _He might be from Arkansas, but he embodies New Hampshire's "Live Free or Die" spirit!_
By joining the two of them on Monday, you’re going to get a look “under the hood” of this tight race for U.S. Senate. And you will be helping Corky end Jeanne Shaheen’s tenure “working” for the Granite State!
Tickets for this event are still available, but availability is limited. Don’t miss out on your chance to reserve your seat at the table! [_**Just click or tap here**_](https://click.mlsend.com/link/c/YT0xNTM0OTc2MzY5MjcwNzI0MTY3JmM9ZjJiNiZlPTE5MjgmYj00MzM2ODkyNzEmZD1kOHAybTFs.uujgEpFVRfCDa5M2n5OQ5ZhO2xjfnjtBRv7snpoayvQ).

name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20Hackett?=
subject: October Surprise
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pat Hackett for Congress
body:
You are our best weapon for influencing the outcome of this election. | | | | [ ](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=1c30bc8538&e=5886ca081d)
---
| | | [ ](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=0e120f7fcf&e=5886ca081d)
---
|
Dear Friend,

Every campaign has a strategy – a path to victory. Jackie Walorski’s strategy has been to refrain from referring to Pat by name. She uses “my opponent” in all her emails and external communications. Why? Because Jackie knows she, herself, is not popular with the majority of voters. Jackie does not want Pat Hackett to have name recognition.

Jackie’s campaign also has tried to sell people on the notion that she has been running a grassroots campaign when we all know her seat is a purchased seat by the insurance, fossil fuel, and gun industries. Jackie’s strategies are losing strategies against you, Pat Hackett, and the people-powered campaign of Dignity and Justice for All.

**We have an October surprise for Jackie and her lobbyist friends in these last two weeks leading up to Election Day – You.**

[**You are our October Surprise.**](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=2ffbfe3c7e&e=5886ca081d) You are our best weapon to influence the outcome of this election. Already, you have been the news event that has caught non-believers in this gerrymandered district off guard for the last two years. We have organized, been resolute and remained faithful to the Hoosier values we know represent Indiana’s 2nd district. Along with Pat spending hundreds of hours traveling the district meeting people in town halls, libraries, pavilions, and homes for Pat Chats, you have made sure Pat Hackett’s name became known throughout all ten counties. When asked, you have phone banked, canvassed, dropped literature, texted, and spoken to your friends and family about Pat and this campaign.

[You are the biggest news event of this campaign. You are our October surprise, and because of you, Dignity and Justice for All will be victorious on November 3rd.](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=7d1d12e2f3&e=5886ca081d)

name: Wesley Hunt for Congress
subject: Hunt for Congress Weekly Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,

Team Hunt has had a tremendous week and I would like to thank the awesome volunteers who turned out for our Get Out The Vote Super Saturday! The outpouring of support we have received from volunteers in the 7th District tells us our message is resonating with the community. In fact, thanks to our volunteers and interns, Team Hunt has knocked over 70,000 doors in the past month. If we remain engaged and active, we can bring positive change to Houston come November!

Thank you to everyone who joined Congressman Dan Crenshaw and me this Monday for a Get Out The Vote Rally featuring candidates throughout Harris County. Early voting began on Tuesday and we are already seeing an incredible turnout from supporters throughout the 7th District. Right now, we need leaders in Washington who will step up to protect the values that created the greatest nation in the history of the world. And as your next Congressman, I pledge to do just that. If you haven't voted yet, I encourage you to do so. Make sure your voice is heard on November 3rd!

This week I had the pleasure of visiting with the hardworking folks at Vermeer Equipment. This great company provides Houston businesses with the equipment they need to build and create opportunity within our city. In Congress, I will work to empower Houston businesses to continue to innovate and create jobs in our communities.

This week I also had the chance to tour Thrustmaster and speak with the great Houstonians they employ. Businesses like Thrustmaster provide opportunity to workers and employers throughout our city. In Congress, I'll work to ensure that businesses like Thrustmaster are protected from government overreach and empowered to continue to grow and innovate.

On Thursday, our campaign [announced a fundraising breakthrough](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=c4e1532f151b49d4bf33ff2666f6f08e&_e=vJrdecm97DIHlyylQ4c4DYyV_LfG3gOTX0fceKTgQAZpZrvQL-biB11vGB4QCnzTFudpj1LmSIyrDT-BBDF7HBCf_XHY6Mwr3n3hqzYUExpC_3GMDJCR2ZItf1RzDsW-R7s0Il4gHRCKW1Y0lgg0WKN3PoZ1hFXw0l3IIY6WBLmgiQJhdq-KfejzmLat_jkFkVw0JOYoUi6YqTKc9C1Wzd2y4AnnUFtXs9zKpXKDQzfcnntOEcNLO0QwHTTY2nQMSqp9IcDbomBXM-dvFT2Oc2-qBq8YiJOf9RM-dyYgnWpJKPaFK_8PXlfIPAyGkdbtuFZx6DacDOdx2fpJwg96Ig%3D%3D): in the third quarter of this year, we more than doubled my opponent's fundraising numbers. Since my wife Emily and I decided to launch this campaign over a year and a half ago, we have been hearing from residents throughout the 7th District that they are ready for change. And these numbers prove we are on the right track. I am humbled that so many Houstonians have shown their trust and support for our campaign.

On Friday, we were excited to announce that our race in the 7th District is now a dead heat. The latest poll of my race against Congresswoman Fletcher shows a [statistical tie between the two of us](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=c4e1532f151b49d4bf33ff2666f6f08e&_e=vJrdecm97DIHlyylQ4c4DYyV_LfG3gOTX0fceKTgQAa-Rx9PUKSVKW_GeS5Po83xg2Z6thh9Ws0ci-_fDIx3CTY6eZ5XgD-bX58H61XDycfd2Cm7Mbbj6J6gcBDZj1kLNPQUooZR38zi0GKxGSA1CCldgbYexSf0iO6kFbsrJ9_B4zN7lai9s6RSn3ono9wZXrSMUK314JxM_oHsDAUg95_URXRMhKOGjgdELh7_HhBdVtCLu_HhXt7XslT8Jp9KLOR0W1EZm5acV8hvI3--gXKEiM_7FEmtgypLu3ULea2emKok2qluPlxRbrTgvQA87EVWoS9lw8MR-h3HJKZhrOmYLqtzBuiNi5eYBRK2_6A%3D). And, with the hard work Team Hunt and our incredible interns and volunteers are putting in, I am more confident than ever before that we are on the right path to succeed in November.

In case you missed it, last week I had the honor of joining Joe Rogan for an episode of his podcast last week. The full episode is now available. You can watch it [here](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=c4e1532f151b49d4bf33ff2666f6f08e&_e=vJrdecm97DIHlyylQ4c4DSt7hbuBb0Pb4WO3_5X_x4TU7JljiukOrsN_qtSTtE3EMhoj7xMJendMsQQ9WsSfvzKpB5DwpvNJRtH4HS7IJ9UnnyS0W8Uk7piUcDWZHy6GEyEFb85XsxE84ot9BJJ2zfgy3ly8PFVtVsGJvU8a4-86lY4D_wQRS11wXbyf2MwpD6cqg0a7VxCe3Q3Cx1Su60nX9ORgF0Iolyi2K8HyJ8flKpALcXsGLhmJTzuCX6lqj0uLMpk4YGrjTJKRNZAno-gcrOn7sqMBB8uK3TYYPus%3D).

Election Day is just 16 days away, and with your help we can bring positive change to Texas' 7th District. Whether it's ensuring our energy workers are protected, securing funding for flood mitigation projects, or improving access to health care for Houston families, I will always fight for Houston and our values first.

I am honored that you've chosen to join Team Hunt. If you're interested in a yard sign for your home or business, please contact us at info@wesleyfortexas.com.

Thank you,
Wesley

****[CONTRIBUTE](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=c4e1532f151b49d4bf33ff2666f6f08e&_e=vJrdecm97DIHlyylQ4c4DZSzPFBf-_pchDDtu8LYmWtfDiI02VWtsqyeqRRJKlS6bD_8g04tbRzjDCWN3OMyUEhZzOuK_wTsa8M0GADwFXG65lQfoGPltGNf6XYg06FIDEI0tQ0ld9mHLGZEEkaaiUrqJkaz6PU4CNogpgpspv5CBiIRRHlqMlflyUWrqrHYxgCkRm-SUUbSOF2PsmqryxxtLBvOKbN0LZkV17rQIPEojlgJWiEiF0RNWzIo-hDk9mDXt7F1P8ckvR2bYipUp3PZ3CRiiufjL_Rx_HxRBZU%3D)****
